meta.title: BoatExperts — Free Marine Electronics Buying Advice From a Certified Tech
meta.description: Not sure which chartplotter, autopilot, or VHF to buy — or if they'll even work together on your boat? Talk to a certified marine electronics tech for free before you spend a dollar.

advisors.name: Mike Torres
advisors.bio: 12 years installing chartplotters, autopilots, and NMEA networks on the Pacific Coast. If it has a screen or a wire, Mike knows it cold.

advisors.name: Jen Calloway
advisors.bio: Former commercial marine tech turned consultant. Specialises in Garmin and Raymarine installs, with a focus on getting mixed systems to talk to each other.

advisors.name: Dave Park
advisors.bio: ABYC-certified electrical technician with 8 years on the Great Lakes. Known for clean installs and zero-mystery wiring diagrams.

hero.headline: Before you buy marine electronics — talk to an expert first.
hero.subheadline: Chartplotters, autopilots, fishfinders, VHF — the wrong combination is an expensive mistake. Get a free session with a certified marine electronics tech who'll tell you exactly what to buy for your boat.
hero.form_note: Free. No commitment. We'll match you with a tech within 24 hours.

benefits.headline: Marine electronics are easy to buy wrong
benefits.subheadline: Compatibility issues, wrong transducer for your hull, displays that don't network — mistakes you only discover after the return window closes.

items.title: Will it fit your helm?
items.body: Display sizes, sunlight readability, mounting depth — what looks great in a YouTube video can be completely wrong for your dash. We'll spec it to your exact helm layout.

items.title: Will it all work together?
items.body: Mixing brands across chartplotter, autopilot, and VHF is where most boaters get stuck. We'll build you a compatible system that shares GPS, depth, and AIS data from day one.

items.title: What do you actually need?
items.body: Marine electronics stores push premium packages. We'll tell you what's genuinely worth buying for how you use your boat — and what you can skip entirely.

how_it_works.headline: How it works
how_it_works.subheadline: Tell us what you're trying to do. We'll build your shopping list.

steps.title: Tell us about your boat and goals
steps.body: Make, model, engine, how you use the water — fishing, cruising, offshore? What do you have now and what are you trying to add or upgrade? Two minutes to fill in.

steps.title: Talk it through with your tech
steps.body: A certified marine electronics tech will walk you through your options on a live video call — what's compatible, what's not, and what actually makes sense for your setup and budget.

steps.title: Get your exact shopping list
steps.body: Before the call ends you'll have a complete, compatible parts list — every unit, cable, transducer, and mounting bracket you need. Nothing missing, nothing wasted.

testimonials.headline: What boaters said after their session

items.quote: I had a Garmin chartplotter in my cart and was about to check out. My tech talked me out of it — wrong transducer for my hull. Saved me $400 and a return headache.
items.name: Chris Flanagan

items.quote: I wanted a fishfinder and an autopilot but had no idea if they'd talk to each other. My tech built me a full Simrad system that networks perfectly. I wouldn't have figured that out alone.
items.name: Amanda Tse

items.quote: Spent 20 minutes on the call and walked away knowing exactly what to order. No guessing, no forum rabbit holes. Just a list and a plan.
items.name: Jorge Medina

form.headline: Get your free electronics consultation
form.subheadline: Tell us your boat and what you're trying to upgrade. Your tech will reach out within 24 hours.
